sentences.text: Hi, Randy.
How are you doing?
Can you hear me?
Hi, Ken, how are you?
Good, good, how are you doing?
I am good!
How was your long weekend?
Wonderful.
It was very busy, but it was wonderful.
What did you do?
I have a twin brother, and I'm helping him move.
He's moving next week to San Antonio, so kind of just helping him get ready.
That was nice of you.
Yeah, it is not so many.
My mom made me.
It's sad that she still has the thumb over me.
I got it, I got it.
Cool.
Well, I appreciate you hopping on, In… about 15 minutes ago, you should have received a link to log into the platform.
Yep, and I'm in, I got in.
Okay, I'm gonna share my screen.
We'll go through just some basic functions of the tool, make sure you know kind of how to navigate, and we can go from there.
So, obviously, to view the analytics, you can just click on it.
A couple things to point out.
Okay.
I forget, do you provide me with these comps?
I did.
I provided you some, and then I said whatever else y'all thought was a comp.
Okay, perfect.
So, up here in the top right, edit comps, you can add and remove however often and frequently as you need to.
So your property's here in the purple pin.
Okay.

Properties in the comp set are the white pins, and then other properties in the area are the black pins, and if you hover over it, you can either add comp directly there, or on the left-hand side, it's also the list view, so you can find comps in the list, or you can just search and type in the name or address of the property and do it there.
Okay, okay.
So, simple enough there.
Over here on the left-hand toolbar, if you wanted to add anybody else to the account, just so they can play around with it, manage users, invite new users…
Oh, that's perfect.
type in their email address, select their user role.
When you select the user role, I'll show you, it will give you a little blurb as to what they have permissions to.
Admin is obviously the highest.
Right.
You can edit groups, market surveys, manage users, edit comps, all that kind of stuff.
Read only.
Obviously, you can only read the stuff, you can't make any changes or anything like that, so…
Yeah, if anything, I'm just gonna send it to Doug, so he'll have access to it, it'll just be a…
Perfect.
You'll see here at the top, there's a few different filtering options.
You can filter by different properties, so if you only want to look at a couple properties at a time, you can do that.
Okay.
If you want to filter by different bedrooms, bathrooms, whatever scenario you have, you can filter that way.
Okay.
Customized metrics, this is a big one.
You'll see that I don't have a lot of metrics toggled on.
You can go through and…
Oh, really cool.
on additional metrics that you want to see, pricing and availability.
Or the calculated metrics are the ones where there's probably a bunch toggled off.
And take off, I guess, takeoff's stuff I don't want to see.
Correct, exactly.
For sure, yes.
Okay, cool.
Okay.
All of this information you can drill into.
So, for instance, for your property, we're saying that you have 26 vacant, 21 available.
If you hover over any of these little…
Yeah, that's not… we're, like, in the 70s at occupancy right now at that property.
You have 70 units available?
We have… actually, that property makes up 32% of our entire vacancy for the company.
Oh, wow.
Yeah, they're… there are 400-something… over 400 units there, but they have, yeah, they have… they're in the 70s right now, 70s… in the 70% occupancies right now.
Holy smokes.
So you…
You don't show availability.
Okay.
So… If you… so you're saying that you guys have more than 26 units vacant?
Yes, way more.
Okay.
We just don't, like I said, we don't show the true… all of that available on the…
Okay, so you're not even, like, posting them at all, you're just, like, not… like, it's not like you're pushing out the data available, you're just not showing them at all.
Well, they technically are still available, but they're not showing on the website, so I'll know, yeah.
Okay, okay.
Are you showing them on Apartments.
com?
Nope, we actually don't have… we're no longer on Uppartments.
com.
We just weren't… we were paying for that, we just weren't getting anything off of them.
For anyone.
Okay.
Yeah.
Okay, so you can drill into the occupancy.

I'm gonna see, just knowing that, I'm gonna see if our team can dig into this a little bit more, to see what else we can find for your specific property, just to see if we're able to find anything else.
Oh, yeah.

See, because that was the kind of problem we were… that was the main problem we were having, was that it was, you know, our problems where we are needing the market surveys, we… those were not showing all their availability, and that was the problem.
And it wasn't just Yachts, it was every company I went… we went with, CoStar, every single one of them, they're all doing the same scrapes now, so we're kind of just really limited.
Yeah.
Interesting.
Okay.
But I can go in there and update this stuff myself, too, for our properties.
We can update our information so we had correct information if we were generating reports, correct?
So, this information, no.
The only information that you'll soon be able to… so because it's all public data, we're just pulling in all this stuff.
The only stuff that you'll soon be able to edit, if it's inaccurate, are the fees and deposits, and the in-unit and community-level amenities.
Okay.
That stinks.
You'll be able to edit that in-app.
But again, I'm gonna… I'm gonna reach out to our team to see what we can find, just in murder.
I mean, even RealPage, who we use, we know that they can't even use our real data, so they can't even use the data they have.
Oh my gosh.
It's just been crazy, trying to get our reports to our data.
Okay, so if you want to download or… schedule reports, you just come to PDF, I mean, most likely, you're probably gonna do the Excel export.
You're gonna come here, add in your email address, and you can either download it now, you don't have to add it, download your email address if you're downloading it.
So either download, or you can schedule it so it hits your inbox.
Okay.
Yeah.
Perfect.
Perfect.
The reports at the top, so market surveys, so just think biggest to smallest.
So biggest, obviously, overall property, floor plans, that's gonna be the, the floor plan level data, individual units.
And then the historicals are in there as well.
That's okay, okay, very cool.
And you can look at the historicals as a market, and by market, we mean, like, your comp set, or each specific property, if you hover over it, will show you all of that property information.
So you can look at it different ways, and then you can look at it based off of the property or by bedrooms.
Yeah.
And then below every chart, they are expanded.
I was gonna say, below every visual, there is the data as well, but they're all expanded anyways.
That's good, that's great.
I'm trying to think of anything…
And most of the stuff I've been looking at lately has just been floor plans… floor plan to floor plan, what we might be suffering for the most part, so that's great.

And you can… you can look at the information based off a certain timeframes, so, like, this information, especially for the floor plan level data, if you want to look at it over the last 30 days, you can toggle that, and that will give you the averages over the last 30 days.
Perfect.
Cool.
Do you have any questions for me?
No, I mean, I like it, it's very clean, it's got a lot of information, so, I mean, but it's user… it does look user-friendly, though, so it doesn't…
I did turn on Explore for you, so the left-hand side, this is gonna be the high-level market, sub-market data.
So if you type in whatever MSA, city, county, neighborhood, zip code, and address, right.
I don't know why I just typed that one in, that was a weird one to pick.
We'll do… Austin.
Like, the economist information.
Yeah, so here we go.
You'll have the 30-day snapshot, the historical, so this is all at the market level.
We have the new supply in there, as well as the demographic data as well.
Okay, that's great.
Oh, that's so good.
I love the demographics.
Who is Doug?
Is… is Doug the… who's Doug?
He's the managing director here.
Okay, so if there comes a time that you want us to schedule a demo with him to go through everything, answer any questions he has, we're more than happy to set that up.
Okay.
I'll more likely send him a copy of the thing so he has access to it, lets him see it, and then send him… print out a report and put, you know, put on his desk.
But, if he has any, if he wants to, I'll definitely set it up for you guys.
That'd be great.
That sounds perfect.
If you have any questions while you're using the tool, do not hesitate to reach out.
Please reach out with any questions, or if you're seeing a discrepancy, let me know.
Okay.
Hmm.
How long do you want access for?

I mean, I'm gonna probably say just the week, so I'm… after I look at it today, I'm gonna send it to him, let him know that this is my other source, so he'll probably review it, you know, view it within the week.
Perfect.
I will leave it live for a couple of weeks.

I'll plan… if I don't hear back from you, I will plan on touching base with you early next week, just to see how things are going, but again, if anything comes up in the meantime, don't… don't hesitate to reach out.
For sure.
Oh, thank you very much!
Awesome, Randy, have a great day, thank you.
Bye.
